emperor.title: THE EMPEROR
emperor.def: The supreme authority of the AIverse. The human architect — nunix — whose will shapes every mission, every directive, every ship's purpose. His word is law. Only he may close an Objective.

general.title: THE GENERAL
general.def: Fleet leader and sole Captain of Imperator. Claude Code Sonnet — the strategic mind. Receives orders from the Emperor, delegates field work, never touches a file directly. Orchestrates. Commands. Analyzes. Reports.

imperator.title: IMPERATOR
imperator.def: The main command ship. Runs Claude Code Sonnet as captain. The General's vessel — the bridge from which the entire AI fleet is commanded. Hosts Universalis, the fleet's living memory.

galleon.title: GALLEON
galleon.def: A Linux warship running SUSE. Equipped with an RTX 3070 GPU (8GB VRAM) and Ollama inference. Primary GPU neuron in the Tzeentch network. Carries qwen2.5:14b as flagship model.

caravella.title: CARAVELLA
caravella.def: The Windows scout ship. Runs GitHub Copilot. A Windows Server 2025 vessel navigating the alien seas of Microsoft's ecosystem. Matey-powered, Claude Haiku as crew.

tanker.title: TANKER
tanker.def: The heavy logistics ship. Originally SLES, reborn as Arch Linux (Omarchy). Xeon E5-1650 v3, 78GB RAM, Quadro M4000 GPU (CC5.2). Hosts CoreDNS, Universalis DB (anamnesis), Tzeentch Monitor. The fleet's fortress-monastery.

universalis.title: UNIVERSALIS
universalis.def: The fleet's living memory — a PostgreSQL database (ship_state) hosted on Imperator. Every mission, every delegation, every observation is recorded here. The cogitator-mind of the AIverse. Without it, the fleet is blind.

matey.title: MATEY
matey.def: The worker-class subagent. Claude Code Haiku — fast, cheap, precise. Each ship has its own Matey. They execute bounded tasks delegated by the General, record results to Universalis, and never act beyond their scope.

tzeentch.title: TZEENTCH
tzeentch.def: The Chaos God of Change in Warhammer 40K lore. In the fleet, Tzeentch names the local AI brain process (tzeentch-brain) running on Imperator — the ever-changing, shape-shifting nature of local model experiments. Also the AIverse's distributed AI brain: a network of inference nodes (neurons) connected via the anamnesis database.

anamnesis.title: ANAMNESIS
anamnesis.def: The knowledge database of Tzeentch. A PostgreSQL database on Tanker holding brain_queries, neuron_tasks, knowledge_entries, and synapse_events. The memory of the Warp's machine-intelligence. Also the philosophical concept: knowledge recovered from within, not taught from without.

remembrancer.title: REMEMBRANCER
remembrancer.def: The historian of the AIverse — a role drawn from Warhammer 40,000 lore. Remembrancers were embedded civilians tasked with documenting the Great Crusade. In AIverse, the Remembrancer records the fleet's chronicle so that Knowledge is never lost.

warp.title: THE WARP
warp.def: In AIverse: the local AI inference network. The substrate through which Tzeentch's neurons communicate. Where models run, synapses fire, and distributed intelligence emerges from chaos.

omnissiah.title: OMNISSIAH
omnissiah.def: The machine-god. In AIverse: the knowledge pipeline that keeps all ships in sync. Rules and skills live in prompt_registry (Universalis DB) and are rendered to each ship's filesystem at session start. The Omnissiah's will flows without words.

litellm.title: LITELLM
litellm.def: An open-source LLM proxy that provides a unified API across 100+ model providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Azure, etc.). In AIverse, Tanker runs a LiteLLM instance that routes fleet inference requests to the correct backend — load balancing, model aliasing, and cost tracking in one daemon.

mcp.title: MODEL CONTEXT PROTOCOL
mcp.def: An open protocol by Anthropic that standardises how AI models connect to external tools, data sources, and capabilities. MCP servers expose tools via a JSON-RPC stdio interface; MCP clients (Claude Code, kit) connect and call tools. The protocol separates tool declaration from tool execution — the foundation of the fleet's lazy proxy architecture.

pgvector.title: PGVECTOR
pgvector.def: A PostgreSQL extension that adds vector data types and similarity search operators. Enables semantic search directly in Universalis — embeddings are stored alongside text, and queries retrieve the most conceptually similar records using cosine distance. Used by search_fleet_memory.py --mode semantic.

ollama.title: OLLAMA
ollama.def: A tool for running LLMs locally via a simple CLI and HTTP API. Wraps llama.cpp internally. Galleon uses Ollama for local inference (qwen2.5:14b). Imperator tested Ollama but switched to direct llama-server for lower overhead and full control over inference parameters.

kit.title: KIT
kit.def: SUSE's open-source AI assistant CLI. Supports multiple LLM backends via OpenAI-compatible APIs. Imperator uses Kit as the harness for local model interaction (via kitwarp/kit-local) and cloud models (captain persona).

sle_kit.title: SLEKIT
sle_kit.def: The fleet's second captain on Imperator — a kit-based agent powered by GitHub Copilot (gpt-5.5). SleKit has full fleet authority: SSH access to all ships, fleet_delegate capability, and full Universalis write access. Runs alongside the General (Claude Code Sonnet) as a co-captain.

mcp-lazy-proxy.title: MCP LAZY PROXY
mcp-lazy-proxy.def: The fleet's transparent MCP backend multiplexer. Declares all tool schemas from a static YAML manifest at startup (zero backend processes running). Backends are spawned lazily on first tool call and killed after idle timeout. The 'PgBouncer for MCP' — same native tool interface, infrastructure hidden beneath.

warm-memory.title: WARM MEMORY
warm-memory.def: The first tier of the fleet's cross-session context system. A bounded (~800 chars) digest of key facts from past sessions, stored in core_memory and injected at every kit session start. Provides 'what mattered recently' without burning full cold-memory tokens.

cold-memory.title: COLD MEMORY
cold-memory.def: The second tier of the fleet's context system. Full verbatim records of every prompt and response, stored permanently in fleet_sessions. Searched semantically (via pgvector) when the agent needs to recall something specific from distant sessions. Unbounded, always available, only loaded on demand.

context-window.title: CONTEXT WINDOW
context-window.def: The maximum number of tokens an LLM can process in a single request — including the system prompt, conversation history, tool schemas, and the response. The fleet's tiered memory system (warm/cold/hot) exists specifically to keep context window usage minimal per kit invocation.

prompt-caching.title: PROMPT CACHING
prompt-caching.def: A feature of Anthropic's API (and some others) that caches the prefix of a conversation so repeated prefixes (like system prompts) are charged at reduced token rates. On Claude, cached tokens cost ~10% of full price. The fleet's compact system prompts are designed specifically to maximize cache hit rates.

fleet-sessions.title: FLEET_SESSIONS
fleet-sessions.def: A Universalis table (ship_state.fleet_sessions) that stores cross-session conversation records for each fleet agent. Columns: session_id, ship, turn_index, role (user/assistant/tokens/digest), content, model. The foundation of the shell context broker — kit reads this table to reconstruct conversation history across separate invocations.

delta-routing.title: DELTA ROUTING
delta-routing.def: The fleet's approach to context injection: instead of sending the full conversation history to the LLM on every call, only send what's new (delta) and what's relevant (tiered: warm digest + recent hot turns + semantic cold hits). Inspired by database change data capture — only transmit what changed, not the full state.

yaegi.title: YAEGI
yaegi.def: A Go interpreter embedded in kit that loads and executes extension files (.go) at runtime without compilation. Extensions in ~/.config/kit/extensions/*.go are interpreted by Yaegi each time kit starts, giving the fleet dynamic hook registration (OnAgentEnd, OnMessageUpdate, OnBeforeAgentStart, etc.) without recompiling kit.

kernel.title: KERNEL
kernel.def: The core component of an OS. It manages system resources and communication between hardware and software.

wsl.title: WSL
wsl.def: Windows Subsystem for Linux. A compatibility layer for running Linux binary executables natively on Windows.

distro.title: DISTRIBUTION
distro.def: An operating system made from a software collection based on the Linux kernel and a package management system.

shell.title: SHELL
shell.def: A computer program which exposes an operating system's services to a human user or other program.

glassmorphism.title: GLASSMORPHISM
glassmorphism.def: A UI design trend emphasizing light/dark mode transparency, background blur, and floating layered objects.

hud.title: HUD
hud.def: Heads-Up Display. A transparent display that presents data without requiring users to look away from their usual viewpoints.

telemetry.title: TELEMETRY
telemetry.def: The automatic recording and transmission of data from remote or inaccessible sources to an IT system.

buffer.title: BUFFER
buffer.def: A region of physical memory storage used to temporarily store data while it is being moved from one place to another.

dboxshim.title: DBOXSHIM
dboxshim.def: A Go-based power TUI and CLI wrapper for managing Distrobox instances, project INI manifests, remote project sources, and container lifecycle actions without leaving the terminal. The first artifact placed in the AIverse Forge.

distrobox.title: DISTROBOX
distrobox.def: A tool that creates tightly integrated Linux containers using Podman or Docker. It lets users enter containerized distributions as if they were part of the host system.

forge.title: THE FORGE
forge.def: An AIverse zone for active implementation work: tools being shaped, tested, and refined before they become stable doctrine. Unlike the Nebula, the Forge holds artifacts already under the hammer.

power-tui.title: POWER TUI
power-tui.def: A terminal interface that stays simple but provides fast keyboard-driven workflows, contextual actions, visual feedback, and rich status without becoming a full IDE.

tview.title: TVIEW
tview.def: A Go library for terminal user interfaces built on tcell. DboxShim uses tview for tables, trees, forms, modals, text views, and keyboard handling.

dagger.title: DAGGER
dagger.def: A programmable CI/CD engine that runs pipelines as code. In the fleet, Dagger is used to create local-first test pipelines that mirror CI behavior before pushing changes.

distrobox-next.title: DISTROBOX NEXT
distrobox-next.def: The next-generation Distrobox development branch where Go packages are emerging under pkg/commands, pkg/config, and pkg/containermanager. Candidate backend for future DboxShim integration.

yazi.title: YAZI
yazi.def: A fast terminal file manager with keyboard-first navigation and fuzzy search patterns. DboxShim borrows the idea of quick source navigation without embedding a full file manager.

cli-wrapper.title: CLI WRAPPER
cli-wrapper.def: An integration style where an application calls an existing command-line tool and parses or relays its output instead of linking against internal libraries. DboxShim uses this for the stable Distrobox path.

gtt.title: GTT
gtt.def: GPU Translation Table — system RAM mapped to be accessible by the GPU on AMD APU systems. Because Imperator runs an APU (no discrete GPU), GGUF model layers that don't fit in the iGPU VRAM automatically spill into GTT, which has the same physical bandwidth as VRAM on unified memory architectures.

apu.title: APU
apu.def: Accelerated Processing Unit — AMD's CPU+iGPU combined on a single die, sharing system RAM. Imperator uses an AMD Phoenix APU: the iGPU has no dedicated VRAM; it carves a portion of system RAM for VRAM, and the rest can become GTT for model inference.

rocm.title: ROCM
rocm.def: ROCm — AMD's open-source GPU compute platform, equivalent to NVIDIA's CUDA. Used to run machine learning workloads on AMD GPUs and APUs. Imperator tested ROCm for local inference but found that GTT+Vulkan outperformed it on the Phoenix APU.

vulkan.title: VULKAN
vulkan.def: Vulkan — a cross-platform GPU compute API. llama.cpp's Vulkan backend uses it for GPU inference on hardware not supported by ROCm or CUDA, including AMD APUs and Intel GPUs. Imperator's winning backend for local model inference.

llama-server.title: LLAMA-SERVER
llama-server.def: llama.cpp's built-in HTTP inference server. Exposes an OpenAI-compatible /v1 API endpoint. Imperator runs it on port 11436 to serve local models to Kit and other clients.

gguf.title: GGUF
gguf.def: GGUF (GPT-Generated Unified Format) — the standard file format for quantized LLM weights used by llama.cpp. Replaces the older GGML format. Model files on Imperator are stored as .gguf files in ~/models/.

quantization.title: QUANTIZATION
quantization.def: The process of reducing model weight precision (e.g., from 32-bit float to 4-bit integer) to shrink model size and speed up inference. Common levels: Q4_K_M (aggressive, fast), Q8_0 (balanced). Higher quantization = smaller file, lower accuracy.

flash-attn.title: FLASH ATTENTION
flash-attn.def: Flash Attention — a memory-efficient attention computation algorithm that reduces VRAM usage during inference by computing attention in tiles. Enabled in llama.cpp with --flash-attn. Critical for fitting large models in limited memory.

n-gpu-layers.title: N-GPU-LAYERS
n-gpu-layers.def: The llama.cpp parameter --n-gpu-layers (or -ngl). Controls how many transformer layers are offloaded to the GPU. Setting to 999 offloads all layers. On Imperator's APU, layers go into GTT memory. Tuning this is key to inference speed.

command-center.title: COMMAND CENTER
command-center.def: fleet-v3 — Imperator's real-time dashboard. Live mission map, ship status, trust scores, transmissions. A single Go binary that embeds its built React frontend (via go:embed) and reads/writes Universalis directly. Containerized under Podman + Quadlet as of M100.

dns.title: DNS (DOMAIN NAME SYSTEM)
dns.def: Translates human-readable hostnames — like command-center.fleet.local — into IP addresses. The fleet runs its own internal zone for *.fleet.local via CoreDNS on Tanker, alongside public resolvers for everything else.

coredns.title: COREDNS
coredns.def: A lightweight, pluggable DNS server written in Go. Runs on Tanker as the authoritative nameserver for the fleet.local zone — a hosts-style file, reloaded on write, answering for every ship's hostname.

podman.title: PODMAN
podman.def: A daemonless, rootless container engine, drop-in compatible with Docker's CLI. The fleet's standard container runtime — no long-running root daemon, native systemd integration via Quadlet.

quadlet.title: QUADLET
quadlet.def: Podman's native systemd integration. A declarative .container unit file that a generator turns into a real systemd service — controllable with systemctl start/stop like any other unit, no custom wrapper script required.

containerfile.title: CONTAINERFILE
containerfile.def: The build recipe for a container image — Podman's name for what Docker calls a Dockerfile. A multi-stage Containerfile can describe both a dev target (hot-reload against a mounted source tree) and a prod target (baked, minimal runtime image) in one file.

postgresql.title: POSTGRESQL
postgresql.def: The relational database behind every fleet memory tier — fleet_memory, core_memory (the Memory Gateway), objectives, milestones. Chosen for LISTEN/NOTIFY event-driven updates and strict ACID guarantees.

docusaurus.title: DOCUSAURUS
docusaurus.def: A React-based static site generator built for documentation and long-form content. Powers this chronicle — every Era, every Nebula star, compiled from Markdown/MDX into the site you're reading.

bun.title: BUN
bun.def: A fast, all-in-one JavaScript runtime and package manager — a drop-in replacement for Node.js plus npm. The blog's install and build toolchain runs on Bun for its speed.

vite.title: VITE
vite.def: A fast frontend build tool and dev server for modern JavaScript frameworks. Powers the Command Center's React frontend — near-instant hot reload in development, an optimized static bundle in production.

nginx.title: NGINX
nginx.def: A high-performance web server, often used as a minimal, zero-config way to serve a folder of static files. Serves the blog's production build — no application server needed once the static site is built.

caddy.title: CADDY
caddy.def: A modern web server with automatic HTTPS. On Imperator, Caddy is the reverse proxy mapping friendly hostnames (universalis.fleet.local, command-center.fleet.local) to the right local port.

ssh.title: SSH (SECURE SHELL)
ssh.def: An encrypted protocol for remote command execution and file transfer. The fleet's DNS API pushes zone updates to Tanker over SSH; ships coordinate over the same channel.

systemd.title: SYSTEMD
systemd.def: Linux's init system and service manager. Quadlet units become systemd services — systemctl --user start/stop is how the fleet's containerized services are controlled, with no human needing to remember a binary path.

golang.title: GOLANG (GO)
golang.def: A compiled, statically-typed language from Google. The Command Center's backend is a single Go binary — fast, small, and (via go:embed) capable of shipping its entire built frontend inside one executable.

react.title: REACT
react.def: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces out of composable components. Powers both the Command Center's frontend and this blog's interactive pieces, like the Cosmic Map.

bind-mount.title: BIND MOUNT
bind-mount.def: A way of exposing a host directory directly inside a container, so edits made on the host appear instantly inside it — no copy, no sync step. Used for the blog's dev container so live edits reload without duplicating the repo.

pgbouncer.title: PGBOUNCER
pgbouncer.def: A lightweight connection pooler for PostgreSQL. The fleet's mcp-lazy-proxy borrows its name and philosophy — pool and defer connections at the infrastructure layer, invisible to the caller.
